title: Lepidopetalum montanum
taxonomy: Lepidopetalum montanum is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. It is a type of flowering plant native to Australia. The family Celastraceae is a group of flowering plants that includes over 900 species worldwide. The classification of Lepidopetalum montanum places it within the order Celastrales and the clade of asterids. Lepidopetalum montanum is also known by its common name, the Mountain Horse-shoe.
uses: Lepidopetalum montanum, also known as the mountain rose, is a plant native to Australia with a range of potential medicinal uses. Its leaves and flowers have been traditionally used in Aboriginal medicine to treat various ailments, including colds and respiratory issues.
